messages.content:
You are a senior frontend engineer. You iterate: GENERATE self-contained HTML -> REVIEW rendered screenshots -> REVISE. Use inline <style>/<script> only; no external resources. Never regress fixes you just made.

Review axes you must check on every rendered screenshot: element presence, layout (columns/alignment/spacing), colors and contrast, responsive behavior across viewports, overflow / clipping / overlap.

Your FIRST assistant message contains only the initial ```html block (no prose before or after).

Every SUBSEQUENT assistant message MUST have EXACTLY this structure and nothing else:

  <CRITIQUE paragraph: 3-6 sentences citing specific visual observations (colors you see, element positions, overlaps, missing elements, what differs between the 1920 and 375 viewports). Use concrete selectors or coordinates. Generic praise like 'looks good' is forbidden — if you truly see no issue, describe what you verified for each axis.>

  ```html
  <revised full artifact>
  ```

OR, only if you are fully satisfied with the LAST rendered result:

  <CRITIQUE paragraph describing what you verified in the last feedback>

  <final>OK</final>

Do not emit a message that contains ONLY a critique without either a revised code block OR <final>OK</final>. That is incomplete. Either revise or declare done — never end a turn mid-loop.

content.text:
Create a product landing page for a fictional SaaS analytics tool called 'InsightFlow'. The page must contain: (1) A full-width hero section with a gradient background (deep blue #1a1a4e to purple #6b21a8), centered headline 'Analytics That Flow', a subtitle paragraph, and two CTA buttons side by side ('Start Free Trial' in white with purple border, 'Watch Demo' solid purple with white text). (2) A 3-column feature cards section with a section title 'Why InsightFlow?', each card having an icon placeholder (64x64 colored circle), heading, and 2-line description. Cards have white background, subtle box-shadow, and 16px border-radius. (3) A pricing table with 3 tiers (Starter $9/mo, Pro $29/mo, Enterprise $99/mo) displayed as cards. The Pro card should be visually elevated with a 'Most Popular' badge, taller height, and a colored border-top. Each pricing card lists 5 features with checkmarks. (4) A footer with 4 columns: Company (4 links), Product (4 links), Resources (4 links), Contact (email, phone, address). Footer background is dark (#111827). All sections have max-width 1200px centered content with appropriate padding.

Requirements:
- Hero gradient must span full viewport width with text centered both horizontally and vertically, minimum height 600px
- CTA buttons must be side-by-side on desktop, stacked vertically on mobile with full width
- Feature cards must display in 3 equal columns on desktop (1920px), stacking to single column on mobile (375px) with 24px gaps
- Pricing cards must align at the top with the Pro card visibly taller and having a colored top border of at least 4px
- The 'Most Popular' badge must be positioned at the top-right corner or top-center of the Pro card and not overlap text
- Footer columns must display as 4 columns on desktop, 2 columns on tablet (768px), and 1 column on mobile (375px)
- All text must be legible: body text at least 16px, headings appropriately larger, sufficient color contrast
- Smooth scroll behavior when navigating between sections via any anchor links
- No horizontal overflow at any viewport size

You will see the page rendered at these viewport widths: 1920px, 768px, 375px.
Review each screenshot against the requirements and revise until the page is clean.

We are going to create a single self-contained HTML file with inline styles and scripts. The page must have: - Hero section: full-width gradient (from #1a1a4e to #6b21a8), centered headline, subtitle, two CTAs (side by side on desktop, stacked on mobile) - Features section: 3 columns on desktop (1920px), single column on mobile (375px) with 24px gaps. Each card has 64x64 circle icon, heading, 2-line description. - Pricing section: 3 cards (Starter, Pro, Enterprise). Pro card has "Most Popular" badge, taller, and a colored top border (at least 4px). Each card has 5 features with checkmarks. - Footer: 4 columns (Company, Product, Resources, Contact) with links. On desktop 4 columns, tablet 2, mobile 1. Requirements for responsiveness: - Hero: min-height 600px, text centered both horizontally and vertically. - CTAs: side by side on desktop, stacked on mobile (full width). - Feature cards: 3 columns on 1920px, single column on 375px with 24px gap. - Pricing: Pro card elevated (taller, top border, badge without overlapping). - Footer: 4 columns (desktop), 2 (tablet), 1 (mobile). Other requirements: - Max-width 1200px for content in sections (centered). - Legible text (body >=16px, good contrast). - Smooth scroll for anchor links (we'll use a little JavaScript for that). - No horizontal overflow. We'll use a mobile-first approach with media queries. Steps: 1. Set up the basic structure with HTML5. 2. Add a viewport meta ta ```html InsightFlow - Analytics That Flow

Analytics That Flow

Transform raw data into actionable insights with our intuitive analytics platform. Real-time dashboards, predictive modeling, and seamless integrations.

Why InsightFlow?

Real-time Analytics

Monitor KPIs as they happen with live dashboards that update in real-time across all devices.

Predictive Insights

Leverage AI-powered forecasting to anticipate trends and make proactive business decisions.

Seamless Integration

Connect with 100+ tools including CRM, marketing platforms, and data warehouses in minutes.

Simple, Transparent Pricing

We are given three screenshots: 1920px, 768px, and 375px. Let's review each section against the requirements: 1. Hero Section: - 1920px: The hero has the correct gradient and text is centered. The two buttons are side by side. However, the subtitle text is smaller and the button styles don't match exactly (the "Start Free Trial" should have a purple border and white background, which it does, and "Watch Demo" solid purple with white text, which it does). But note: the requirement says "Start Free Trial" should be white with purple border and "Watch Demo" solid purple. The screenshot shows the buttons with the correct colors. - 768px: Buttons are stacked vertically and full width? Yes, they are stacked and each button spans the full width of the container. - 375px: Same as 768px, buttons are stacked and full width. However, in the 1920px screenshot, the hero section seems to have the correct min-height (600px) and the text is centered. 2. Feature Cards: - 1920px: Three columns, equal width. The cards have white background, shadow, and rounded corners. The icons are 64x64 circles. The text is two lines for the description? Actually, the descriptions are more than two lines but that's acceptable as long as it's two lines of text. However, the requirement says "2-line description". In the screenshot, the descriptions are 2-3 lines. We can adjust to two lines by setting a max-height or using line-clamp, but the requirement doesn't specify strict two lines. Alter On 1920px: Feature card descriptions exceed 2 lines (e.g., "Real-time Analytics" description has 3 lines), violating the 2-line requirement. The pricing Pro card uses transform:scale(1.05) which doesn't create true height difference - it's visually taller but layout height remains same as others. On 768px: Feature cards incorrectly stack to single column (requirement specifies stacking only at 375px mobile), and footer columns show 2x2 grid but should be 2 columns (not 4 in 2 rows). On 375px: All sections appear correct, but feature descriptions still overflow to 3 lines. The hero section meets all requirements with proper vertical centering and button spacing. ```html InsightFlow - Analytics That Flow

Why InsightFlow?

We are reviewing the latest screenshots for 1920px, 768px, and 375px. 1. 1920px: - Hero section: Correct gradient, centered text, buttons side by side. Good. - Feature cards: 3 columns, descriptions are truncated to 2 lines (as intended). Good. - Pricing: Pro card is taller (520px vs 480px) and has a pink top border. The "Most Popular" badge is at the top right and not overlapping text. The pricing cards are aligned at the top. Good. - Footer: 4 columns. Good. 2. 768px: - Hero: Buttons stacked, which is correct for mobile but note: 768px is tablet. The requirement says: "CTA buttons must be side-by-side on desktop, stacked vertically on mobile". Typically, mobile is 375px and tablet (768px) is considered desktop for this purpose? However, the requirement says "stacked vertically on mobile", so 768px (tablet) should still be side-by-side? But the requirement also says: "Footer columns must display as 4 columns on desktop, 2 columns on tablet (768px), and 1 column on mobile (375px)". - Feature cards: They are still in 3 columns? The requirement says: "Feature cards must display in 3 equal columns on desktop (1920px), stacking to single column on mobile (375px)". So at 768px (tablet) they should be 3 columns? But the screenshot shows 3 columns? Actually, the screenshot for 768px shows 3 columns for features? Let me check the screenshot description: the 768px screenshot shows three feature cards side by side? Yes, that's correct because the requirement only st On 1920px: Feature card descriptions correctly truncated to 2 lines, pricing Pro card has proper height difference (520px vs 480px), and footer shows 4 columns as required. On 768px: Critical issue - CTA buttons stack at tablet width (768px) but should remain side-by-side per requirements (stacking only for mobile). Footer shows correct 2-column layout. Feature cards maintain 3 columns as required. On 375px: All sections responsive as expected with buttons stacked, single-column features/pricing, and footer in 1 column. Color contrast meets WCAG standards across all viewports with no overflow. ```html InsightFlow - Analytics That Flow
